text: ILLNESS /c69Calcium is needed for growth and bone development. /c69Children require more calcium. /c69Calcium prevents osteoporosis (thinning of bones). /c69Milk, curds and nuts are rich sources of bio-available calcium (Ragi and GLV are also good dietary sources of calcium). /c69Regular exercise reduces calcium loss from bones. /c69Exposure to sunlight maintains vitamin D status which helps in calcium absorption. /c93Never starve the child. /c93Feed energy-rich cereals-pulse diet with milk and mashed vegetables. /c93Feed small quantities at frequent intervals. /c93Continue breast-feeding as long as possible. /c93Give plenty of fluids during illness. /c93Use oral rehydration solution to prevent and correct dehydration during diarrhea episodes. 37 Table 5 WHO New Growth Standards Standard
